summary: Lex Fridman interviews Zach Bitter, an ultramarathon runner and coach, about the mental and physical challenges of ultramarathon running, including strategies for managing negative thoughts, training methodologies, and the role of nutrition, specifically low-carb and meat-based diets, in endurance performance. Bitter also discusses his upcoming run across America and his experiences with different types of running challenges.

key_details.fact: Zach Bitter is training for a run across America planned for September.
Bitter advises running a 100-mile race for the first time to experience the full range of emotions and mental processes.
David Goggins uses an approach of separating his mind into a weak and strong version, while Sam Harris advocates observing thoughts and letting them go.
The MAF 180 formula (180 minus age) is a good starting point for determining aerobic threshold, but individual adjustments may be needed.
Maximum Aerobic Function training involves consistent training at a high volume with low injury risk, prioritizing fat metabolization.
Zach Bitter had his best racing season in 2019, during which he emphasized the big picture over individual events.
The world record for the 100-mile run was 11:14:56, set by Alex Sorkin, with a pace of 6:45 per mile.
Zach Bitter's 100-mile world record on the treadmill was prompted by race cancellations in March 2020.
Zach Bitter plans to document his run across America on Instagram and potentially YouTube.
Zach Bitter aims to average 72 miles per day during his run across America, with a goal of completing it in 42 days.

topics: The role of suffering in endurance sports
